notes: Three kiro-cli lifetimes back-to-back, one per session-end variant: (1) clean /quit after a no-op turn, (2) SIGKILL while idle in ready, (3) SIGKILL mid-turn while working. Each variant asserts a ready→working→ready arc then process_exited and disappearance. Each variant's process_exited anchors the next variant's birth. Process exit deletes the session by design (kiro-cli matched by ExactName + cwd; pgrep stops matching the dead parent). Observations: kiro-cli JSONL carries no model/token/cost (FINDINGS.md §4), so model is 'unknown' and cost/tokens are zero.

text: Variant 1: first user prompt drives ready → working
Variant 1: turn completes back to ready
Variant 1: clean /quit — process_exited fires AFTER state returned to ready (the v1_turn_done → v1_exit chain proves 'State is ready at exit')
Variant 2: new session appears in ready after variant 1 finishes
Variant 2: user prompt drives ready → working
Variant 2: turn completes back to ready (idle window begins)

Variant 2: SIGKILL mid-idle — process_exited fires while session is in ready; no transition to working or waiting between v2_turn_done and process_exited (satisfies 'state never entered working post-exit')
Variant 3: new session appears in ready after variant 2 finishes
Variant 3: user prompt drives ready → working

Variant 3: SIGKILL mid-turn — process_exited fires while session is in working; the live sweep-to-ready transition may not appear in the recording's 8s tail (recipe caveat), so this anchors to v3_turn_start rather than requiring an explicit post-crash ready phase
